index.title: LingoDesk — Chat with the world in your native language on Telegram
index.desc: LingoDesk is an automatic translation relay for Telegram Business: incoming messages arrive translated into your language, and your replies go out in theirs — sent as you.
index.og_title: LingoDesk — Chat with the world in your native language
index.og_desc: An automatic translation relay for Telegram Business. Receive → translate → reply → translate, sent as you.
index.badge: Free plan · Open source · Cancel anytime
index.hero_h1: Chat with the world in your native language
index.hero_sub: Customers write to your personal Telegram in any language. It lands translated into yours; you reply in yours — it reaches them in theirs, sent as you. Fully automatic.
index.cta_note: Free to start, no server or code. Go Pro anytime, or self-host the open source.
index.mock_translation: Hi, how much is the full package?
index.mock_reply: Package A is $100 and includes everything.
index.how_lead: One dedicated topic per contact in your private console group. Nothing changes on their side — they just see you, fluent.
index.step1_p: Spanish, Russian, Arabic — any language, straight to your personal account. LingoDesk listens silently.
index.step2_p: Original and translation side by side in a bilingual card, inside that contact's own topic.
index.step3_p: Type naturally. AI translates; you see a preview and nothing is sent until you approve it.
index.step4_p: They see your own account replying fluently in their native tongue. No bot in sight.
index.feat1_h: Your real account, your own bot
index.feat1_p: Built on the official Telegram Business channel with a bot you create and can revoke any time. Token sovereignty, by design.
index.feat2_p: Detected from the first message, targeted on every reply. One command to correct it — or to switch your own language.
index.feat3_p: Each translation is shown to you first and goes out only on your confirmation. Accidental sends are structurally impossible.
index.feat4_p: Messages, photos and files archived in both directions — original next to translation, retrievable any time.
index.cases_lead: Faithful re-creations of the actual product UI — this is exactly what you'll see once you're set up.
index.case1_h: Incoming message, bilingual card in seconds
index.case1_p: Every customer gets their own topic — original and translation arrive together, nothing to look up.
index.case2_h: Nothing goes out without your OK
index.case2_p: Type in your language, review the translation, tap send — it goes out from your own account.
index.case3_tr: Can we move the meeting to Friday?
index.case3_my: No problem, Friday at 10 AM works.
index.case3_p: Add your bot to any group or channel and send /glang once — their messages are translated for you, yours are translated for them. Everyone just speaks their own language.
index.proof_note: We're user #1 — LingoDesk runs our own cross-border business every day. These are live production numbers, not marketing copy.
index.pricing_title: Free to start. Pro when you scale.
index.pricing_lead: No server, no code — set up from your phone in ~10 minutes. Or self-host the open source, free forever.
index.free_li4: Translation engine included, no API keys
index.pro_li4: Pay in Telegram Stars · cancel anytime
index.self_li1: Your machine, your data — full control
index.self_li4: Zero lock-in, graduate from hosted anytime
index.final_p: From creating your bot to your first cross-language reply — every step illustrated, every pitfall flagged.
about.title: About & Contact — LingoDesk
about.desc: LingoDesk lets anyone chat and do business across languages on Telegram — sent as you, fully automatic. Get in touch: hello@lingodesk.org
about.hero_tag: Chat and do business with the world — in your native language.
about.build_p1: LingoDesk turns your personal Telegram into a cross-language desk. Customers write to you in any language; every message lands translated into yours, in a tidy per-contact thread. You reply in your own language — it's translated back and sent as you, automatically. The other side never sees a bot; they just see you, fluent in their tongue.
about.build_p2: It's built for freelancers, cross-border sellers and small teams who close deals in chat — people for whom a missed or clumsy reply is a lost customer.
about.diff_1: Your own bot, your token. Everything runs through a bot you create and can revoke any time — full control, no black box.
about.diff_2: Open source, no lock-in. MIT-licensed. Let us host it, or run it on your own server — same code, your choice.
about.diff_3: Small and focused. An independent product built by people who care about cross-border communication, not growth-at-all-costs.
about.contact_note: Questions, feedback, partnerships, or help getting set up — we read every email.
changelog.i10_p: Translation now runs on a three-tier engine chain — Claude as primary with two automatic fallbacks. If the primary has an outage, a circuit breaker switches over in seconds and switches back when it recovers. Your conversations never stop.
changelog.i9_h: Reply templates, AI drafts, group translation & usage dashboard
changelog.i9_p: Save frequent replies and send them with /t. Let AI draft a reply from the conversation with /draft. Add your bot to any group or channel for two-way live translation with /glang. Check your monthly usage anytime with /usage.
changelog.title: Changelog — LingoDesk
changelog.desc: What's new in LingoDesk — features, improvements and updates.
changelog.hero_tag: Features, improvements and fixes as they ship.
changelog.i1_p: The website and bot are going multilingual — Spanish, Portuguese, Russian, Arabic, French, Indonesian, Vietnamese, Thai, Turkish, Hindi, Bengali, Urdu and more — so you can use LingoDesk in your own language.
changelog.i2_p: A Free plan (300 replies/month) and Pro (500 ⭐/month, unlimited), paid natively in Telegram Stars, cancel anytime.
changelog.i3_p: DM your bot token to @LingoDeskbot and you're live in seconds — no email, no waiting.
changelog.i4_p: No need to wait for customers to message first — reach out from your own account and they appear in your console automatically.
changelog.i5_p: A cleaner, faster site with an illustrated, step-by-step guide that covers every common pitfall.
changelog.i6_p: Multi-tenant by design: every user runs their own bot with full token control.
changelog.i7_p: Self-host on your own server, or let us host it — same code, zero lock-in.
changelog.i8_p: Receive → translate → reply → translate — chat with the world in your native language, fully automatic.
setup.title: Quick Start — LingoDesk Complete Setup Guide
setup.desc: From zero to running: create the bot in BotFather, link it in Telegram Business, set up the console forum group, configure .env and send your first cross-language reply — every step illustrated.
setup.hero_h1: Quick start: from zero to running, no steps skipped
setup.hero_p: The default path is cloud-hosted: just a phone and ~10 minutes — no server, no code. Every step tells you which screen to open and what to tap, plus the pitfalls people actually hit.
setup.toc_s1: Step 1 · Create your bot in BotFather (two critical switches)
setup.toc_s2: Step 2 · Link the bot in Telegram Business
setup.toc_s3: Step 3 · Create your console forum group
setup.prep_1: Telegram Premium — the Telegram Business features LingoDesk relies on (message relay to a bot) are Premium-only. Without Premium none of this works.
setup.prep_2: A phone — that's all the cloud-hosted mode (the default) needs: do Steps 1–3 on your phone, send us the token, done.
setup.prep_3: Self-hosting only: Node.js ≥ 20 (or Docker), an Anthropic API key, and an always-on computer/server. The code is free and open source on GitHub.
setup.s1_intro: In Telegram, search for @BotFather (look for the blue verification badge) and have this conversation:
setup.s1_li1: The "name" is just a display name; the "username" must be globally unique and end in bot — if taken, try another.
setup.s1_li2: That digits:letters string in the last reply is your BOT_TOKEN. Copy it somewhere safe. It is effectively the bot's password — never share it.
setup.s1a_h: 1a. Turn on Secretary Mode (required; formerly "Business Mode")
setup.s1a_p: ⚠️ In May 2026 Telegram renamed this switch to Secretary Mode; older clients may still label it Business Mode — same thing. Do this inside the @BotFather chat (not your bot's profile page, and not your phone's Telegram settings). Two interfaces — either works:
setup.s1a_iface1: Interface ① Classic commands — just send messages:
setup.s1a_iface2: Interface ② New management panel — tap the blue Open button next to BotFather's message box:
setup.s1a_callout_ok: Can't find it? ① It has two names: Secretary Mode in new clients, Business Mode in older ones — look for either; ② make sure you're inside @BotFather, not your own bot's settings; ③ the toggle has nothing to do with Telegram Premium — Premium only matters for linking on your account side in Step 2; anyone can flip this switch.
setup.s1a_callout_warn: Without Secretary Mode, Telegram shows a popup — "This bot doesn't support Telegram Business yet" — when you try to add it in Step 2. It's the single most common blocker. Flip the switch: it takes effect instantly, no need to recreate the bot; just retry adding it.
setup.s1b_callout_warn: Privacy is on by default, and while it's on the bot cannot see the replies you type in your console group — the symptom is "I type in the topic and nothing happens". Turn it off (BotFather must say Privacy mode is disabled).
setup.s2_h: Link the bot in Telegram Business
setup.s2_p: On the mobile Telegram app (this setting currently exists only on mobile):
setup.s2_note: In newer clients this item is called "Chat Automation" — different label, same entry.
setup.s2_li1: Type your bot's username (e.g. my_lingodesk_bot) in the search box and select it.
setup.s2_li2: Permissions: enable "Reply to messages" — without it the bot can read but can never send on your behalf.
setup.s2_li3: For "which chats", choose All 1:1 Chats; add any private contacts you want to keep out of the relay to the Excluded Chats list.
setup.s2_callout_ok: Once linked, every private message you receive is silently "cc'd" to the bot — the other side notices nothing, and your own chat experience doesn't change at all.
setup.s3_p: This group is your workbench: one topic per contact, with bilingual message cards popping up inside.
setup.s3_li1: Create a group: New → New Group, add your bot as the only member (search its username). Name it anything, e.g. "LingoDesk Console".
setup.s3_li2: Enable Topics: open the group → tap the name for its profile → Edit → toggle Topics on. The group upgrades to a supergroup and gets a topic list.
setup.s3_li3: Promote the bot to admin: profile → Administrators → Add Admin → your bot → check "Manage Topics" (leave the rest default) → save.
setup.s3_callout_warn: The bot creates a topic automatically for each contact. Without admin rights + "Manage Topics" that fails — the symptom is "a customer wrote to me but nothing appears in the group".
setup.s3_callout_ok: The group only needs you and the bot — no other humans. If you add a partner, their replies in a topic trigger translation and sending just like yours.
setup.s4_p: Everything on the phone side is done. Now choose your path — no server? Pick A: nothing to install.
setup.s4_a_h: ☁️ Option A · Cloud-hosted (recommended: no server, no code, instant)
setup.s4_a_li1: DM the official activation bot @LingoDeskbot, send /start, then paste the BOT_TOKEN from Step 1 right into the chat.
setup.s4_a_li2: Instant automatic activation: once the token validates it replies "🎉 Activated" (and deletes your token message). The Free plan covers 300 translated replies/month; upgrade to Pro (500 ⭐/mo) anytime for unlimited. Translation engine is on us — no API keys to sign up for.
setup.s4_a_li3: Follow its reply to finish linking, then send /bind in your console group — the bot replies "✅ bound" and you're done. Jump to Step 5 to test. DM it /status any time, or /native en to set your own language.
setup.s4_a_callout_ok: Worried about handing over the token? It's stored encrypted and only used to relay the chats you authorized in Telegram Business — and you stay in control: revoke it any time in BotFather (/mybots → API Token → Revoke) and the old token dies instantly.
setup.s4_b_h: 💻 Option B · Self-host (your own machine, your own data)
setup.s4_b_p: First grab a translation key at console.anthropic.com → API Keys → Create Key (sk-ant-…; a few dollars of credit lasts a long time at ~$0.001 per message). Then:
setup.s4_b_p2: Once you see the banner, send /bind in your console group to link it (group ID and your user ID are detected automatically — no numbers to look up). On a server, Docker is one command: docker compose up -d — see the GitHub README.
setup.s4_b_callout_warn: One bot token = one running instance, globally (409 Conflict = an old process is still holding it). If we host you, don't also run a local instance.
setup.s5_p: From a different Telegram account (a second account or a friend), send a foreign-language DM to your personal account (not to the bot!) — say, "Hola, ¿cuánto cuesta?" in Spanish. Within seconds, a new topic named after the sender should appear in your console group:
setup.s5_chat_bot1:
🆕 New contact @amigo_es (es)
Original: Hola, ¿cuánto cuesta?
🌐 Hi, how much does it cost?
─────
↳ Type here in your language — it goes out in es, sent as you
setup.s5_chat_me: Package A is $100 and includes all features.
setup.s5_chat_bot2:
📤 Translation preview (→es), sent only after you confirm:

El paquete A cuesta 100 dólares e incluye todas las funciones.

(Your message: Package A is $100…)

[ ✅ Send to customer ] [ ✏️ Cancel ]
setup.s5_after: Tap "✅ Send to customer" — the other side sees your personal account replying in fluent Spanish. The whole loop is now live 🎉
setup.s5_li1: Photos, files and voice messages they send are forwarded into the topic and archived automatically.
setup.s5_li2: Wrong language detected? Send /lang es in the topic to correct it; /help lists all commands.
setup.s5_li3: Replying manually from your phone is fine too — the bot records it into the topic archive.
setup.s5_li4: You can start conversations too — no need to wait for them. Message anyone from your real account (even just "hi") and they instantly appear in the console with a topic created; from then on, type in your language here to keep chatting with them.
setup.faq_q1: A customer wrote to me but nothing appears in the console group
setup.faq_a1: Check in order: ① BotFather → Business Mode is turned on (Step 1a); ② Telegram Business → Chatbots has your bot linked and that chat is within scope (Step 2); ③ the bot is a group admin with "Manage Topics" (Step 3); ④ the console group is bound — send /bind in it; ⑤ self-hosters: the bot process is still running without errors.
setup.faq_q2: Popup when adding the bot: "This bot doesn't support Telegram Business yet"
setup.faq_a2: Business Mode isn't enabled in BotFather (Step 1a). Go to @BotFather → /mybots → your bot → Bot Settings → Business Mode → Turn on. It takes effect instantly — no need to recreate the bot or resubmit the token; just go back to Telegram Business → Chatbots and add it again. Cloud-hosted users can DM @LingoDeskbot /status to double-check this item.
setup.faq_q3: I send /bind in the group and nothing happens
setup.faq_a3: ① Group Privacy is still on (Step 1b) — turn it off, then remove and re-add the bot; ② Topics aren't enabled on the group; ③ cloud-hosted users: make sure @LingoDeskbot replied "Activated" (DM it /status to check); ④ self-hosters: make sure the process is running.
setup.faq_q4: Is it safe to hand over my BOT_TOKEN for cloud hosting?
setup.faq_a4: The token is stored encrypted and only used to relay chats within the scope you authorized in Telegram Business — and you can exclude private contacts (Step 2). You stay in control: revoke it any time via BotFather → /mybots → API Token → Revoke; the old token dies instantly and the service stops. To resume, just send the new token to @LingoDeskbot.
setup.faq_q5: I type in a topic and the bot does nothing
setup.faq_a5: Nine times out of ten it's Group Privacy still on (Step 1b). BotFather → Bot Settings → Group Privacy → Turn off, then remove the bot from the group and re-add it (the change only applies after rejoining), and promote it to admin again.
setup.faq_a6: Two instances are running on the same token. Find and stop the old process (Ctrl+C or pkill -f "tsx.*main.ts"), wait ten seconds, start again.
setup.faq_q7: "Send to customer" fails with a generic error
setup.faq_a7: Check in order of likelihood: ① the "Reply to messages" permission is off (most common) — Settings → Telegram Business → Chat Automation (Chatbots) → your bot → enable "Reply to messages"; without it the bot can read but never send; ② the customer's chat isn't within the bot's accessible-chats scope (same page); ③ the contact hasn't messaged you in over 24 hours — Telegram blocks bot-initiated sends; ask them to send anything first. Cloud-hosted users: DM @LingoDeskbot /status — the "reply permission" line gives you the answer directly.
setup.faq_q8: "Send to customer" fails: contact inactive too long
setup.faq_a8: A Telegram restriction: if a conversation has been idle too long, the bot can't initiate on your behalf (BUSINESS_PEER_USAGE_MISSING). Ask them to send anything to reactivate, or reply manually from your phone this once.
setup.faq_a9: Go to Settings → Telegram Business → Chatbots, remove and re-add the bot — or simply wait for the customer's next message, which lets the bot capture a fresh connection automatically.
setup.faq_q10: Wrong language detected / want a different target language
setup.faq_a10: The language locks on the contact's first message (to avoid flip-flopping on short texts). Send /lang code in that contact's topic to correct it, e.g. /lang pt (Portuguese) or /lang ar (Arabic).
setup.faq_q11: After a restart, old "Send / Cancel" buttons stopped working
setup.faq_a11: Pending previews live in memory and expire on restart (the button will tell you). Just type your reply again in the topic. Contacts and chat history live in the database — nothing is lost.
setup.done_p: From now on, customers write in whatever language they like — you just type in yours. If something breaks, run through the troubleshooting list above first; still stuck, email us with a screenshot of the terminal error.
